mnemonics.title: DBMS Key Purposes
mnemonics.content: Every Organized Database Manager Uses Systematic Tables: EODMUST - Extensibility, Organized data, Data independence, Management, User interface, Security, Transaction management, Undo/Recovery, Standardized queries

acronyms.usage: ANSI is the body that standardized the three-level database architecture.
SPARAC developed the three-schema architecture for databases.
DBA is responsible for managing and maintaining the database system.
DDL is used to define database schema (CREATE, ALTER, DROP).
DML is used to manipulate data in the database (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
DCL is used for access control (GRANT, REVOKE).

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pyramid with three layers: Top (External - individual views), Middle (Conceptual - whole organization view), Bottom (Internal - physical storage). The user sees only the top, while physical details are at the bottom.

Imagine a sandwich with three layers - changing the bottom bread (physical storage) shouldn't affect the top filling (user view). Logical independence means middle layer changes don't affect top layer.

Use a library analogy - file system is like messy personal bookshelf with duplicate books everywhere; DBMS is like a well-organized library with catalog, one copy of each book, and a librarian managing access.

analogies.analogy: Like a company's organizational structure - employees see only their department (External), managers see the whole department structure (Conceptual), and the building maintenance team knows exactly where everything is stored (Internal).
Like a dictionary in a book - it contains information ABOUT the content (definitions, meanings) rather than the content itself. Similarly, data dictionary stores metadata about the database.
Like a hotel manager - manages resources (rooms/data), ensures security, handles problems, coordinates between guests (users) and staff (system).
